questions.questionText: The Forex market is best described as:
options: A physical exchange located in New York
A marketplace that only operates during US business hours
questions.explanation: Forex has no central exchange; it’s a global OTC network operating 24/5.

questions.questionText: Approximately how much is traded in Forex every day?
questions.explanation: Daily Forex volume is roughly $7.5T, the largest market on Earth.

questions.questionText: A trader who believes EUR/USD will fall would:
options: Buy US Dollars on the stock market
Wait until EUR/USD rises before trading
questions.explanation: To profit from a fall, you short the pair: sell first, buy back lower.

questions.questionText: Which would most likely cause a currency to strengthen?
options: The central bank raising interest rates
questions.explanation: Higher rates attract capital flows into that currency, increasing demand.

questions.questionText: Why do most retail Forex traders lose money (per the lesson)?
options: The market is rigged against retail
Forex is impossible to trade profitably
Retail traders have less money than banks
Lack of education, structure, and emotional discipline
questions.explanation: The consistent failure is usually approach: no system, no risk plan, emotion-driven.

questions.questionText: Which participant sets the macro backdrop via interest-rate policy?
questions.explanation: Central banks drive rate policy and can move FX markets violently.

questions.questionText: A corporation converting overseas revenue back to home currency is:
questions.explanation: Corporations trade FX mainly to hedge and settle real-world cash flows.

questions.questionText: A stop hunt is best described as:
options: Retail traders targeting each other’s stops
A move that triggers clustered stops to provide liquidity for large orders
questions.explanation: It’s a liquidity mechanic: stops are orders; triggering them creates fillable liquidity.

questions.questionText: Which broker type can take the opposite side of your trade (dealing desk)?
questions.explanation: Market makers internalize your flow; ECN/STP route to external liquidity.

questions.questionText: Retail volume is roughly what share of total daily Forex volume?
questions.explanation: Most volume is institutional; retail is small but highly predictable.

questions.questionText: In GBP/JPY, which is the base currency?
questions.explanation: Base is always the left side of the slash.

questions.questionText: EUR/USD falls from 1.1200 to 1.0900. What happened?
questions.explanation: If the pair falls, the base (EUR) is getting cheaper in quote (USD).

questions.questionText: If EUR/USD is 1.0845 / 1.0848 and you want to SELL, you execute at:
questions.explanation: Sell at BID, buy at ASK.

questions.questionText: Why are exotic pairs not recommended for beginners?
options: Wide spreads, lower liquidity, and higher event/political risk
questions.explanation: Cost + slippage + tail risk makes them unforgiving during learning.
